phase_title: Helper extension — Cpp declaration entry arm + descend_kinds Cpp branch

edits.location: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (outer fn) — placed BEFORE the OCaml application_expression arm and BEFORE the generic args-list lookup at L4076
edits.summary: New Cpp arm: when `language == Language::Cpp && descendant.kind() == "declaration"`, walk the descendant's named children for an `init_declarator`, look up its `value` field (= `argument_list`), then delegate to `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent` (depth=0). Returns Some(identifier) on first identifier-shaped leaf via leftmost-source-order BFS; returns None if no init_declarator/value found.
edits.rationale: tree-sitter-cpp 0.23.4 parses `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);` as `declaration → init_declarator { value: argument_list { binary_expression { left: call_expression(std::stringstream → identifier(d)) } } }`. The descendant matched by detect_sinks_ast for the boost::archive::text_iarchive Deserialize sink is the `declaration` node. `declaration` has no `arguments` field and `init_declarator` does not match `kind.contains("argument")`, so the generic args-list lookup at L4076 returns None and var-extraction silently drops the source/sink pair. The new entry arm short-circuits before L4076 and reuses the existing depth-bounded BFS descent helper (no body change to the descent helper itself). The descent helper applies the per-level `string_node_kinds(language)` filter at every recursion step, so closes-#24 string-literal regression-guard is preserved by construction.

edits.location: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (outer fn) — descend_kinds match arm (was L4117-4129)
edits.summary: Extend the per-language `descend_kinds` match arm with a `Language::Cpp` branch listing `["binary_expression", "call_expression", "parenthesized_expression", "argument_list"]`. Updated the surrounding doc-comment to remove the var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 deferral note and add a clarification that this Cpp descend-through set is forward-coverage for future Cpp call_expression sinks whose first arg is a nested constructor / parenthesised / binary expression — the cpp_deserialization_positive flow does NOT consult this path (it short-circuits via the new entry arm above, before reaching the L4076 args-list lookup).
edits.rationale: Plan-mandated forward-coverage extension. Per premortem amendment A1 / discrepancy DI-1, this is COSMETIC for cpp_deserialization_positive but PROVIDES PROTECTION for future Cpp call_expression nested-constructor sink shapes. Mirrors the Java + Scala descend_kinds entries already present (commit b577796 var-extract-nested-constructor-v1).

edits.summary: Updated doc-comment to remove the `cpp_deserialization_positive deferred to cpp-deser-declaration-v1 follow-on` deferral note and replace with a brief reference to how the Cpp `declaration` entry arm in the OUTER helper now delegates here. Body unchanged (still unconditional BFS over named descendants with depth bound MAX_DEPTH=5 and per-level `string_node_kinds(language)` filter at L4208).
edits.rationale: Doc-comment hygiene. Body is unchanged — verified by the absence of a body-edit in the diff.

no_changes: CPP_AST_SINKS bank (already has boost::archive::text_iarchive Deserialize entry at L2658)
TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType / VulnType variants — none added
Predecessor helpers (var-extract-nested-constructor-v1, ruby-backtick-extraction-v1, rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1, php-echo arm, ocaml application_expression arm)
static_assertions: Language::Cpp gating on the new outer-helper arm guarantees no cross-language pollution (per premortem ✓ VERIFIED claim 5).
The descent helper's per-level `string_kinds.contains(&cur.kind())` filter at L4208 preserves closes-#24 by construction at every recursion level.
Entry arm short-circuits with `return None` if no init_declarator / value found — fail-closed under tree-sitter-cpp grammar drift.
